title: Day 1 - Backend Setup & API
body:
## 📌 Objective
Node.js Express server set up karein. Environment configuration add karein aur ek API endpoint `POST /api/rooms` banayein jo unique 9-digit room ID (XXX-XXX-XXX) return kare.

## ✅ Tasks
- [ ] Node.js project initialize karein (`npm init`)
- [ ] Express.js install aur server entry point banayein (`server.js`)
- [ ] `dotenv` setup karein aur `.env` file configure karein (PORT, NODE_ENV)
- [ ] `POST /api/rooms` endpoint implement karein
- [ ] Unique 9-digit room ID generate karne ka logic likhein (format: XXX-XXX-XXX)
- [ ] Basic error handling middleware add karein
- [ ] Server health-check endpoint add karein (`GET /api/health`)

## 🎯 Acceptance Criteria
- Server successfully starts on configured PORT
- `POST /api/rooms` returns a unique room ID in XXX-XXX-XXX format
- Environment variables `.env` file se load hoti hain
- Duplicate room ID generate na ho

title: Day 2 - Socket.IO & Room State Management
body:
## 📌 Objective
Socket.IO server initialize karein. Room state management code likhein taaki ek room me max 2 users hi connect ho sakein (One-to-One baseline enforce karne ke liye).

## ✅ Tasks
- [ ] `socket.io` package install karein aur Express server ke saath integrate karein
- [ ] In-memory room state store banayein (Map ya Object)
- [ ] Socket `connection` aur `disconnection` events handle karein
- [ ] Room join logic implement karein: room exists check, user count check
- [ ] Max 2 users per room restriction enforce karein
- [ ] Room full hone par client ko `room-full` error event emit karein
- [ ] User disconnect hone par room state clean up karein
- [ ] CORS configuration Socket.IO ke liye add karein

## 🎯 Acceptance Criteria
- Socket.IO server successfully initialize ho
- Ek room mein sirf 2 users hi join kar sakein
- Teesra user join karne ki koshish kare toh `room-full` error mile
- User disconnect hone par room state correctly update ho
- Empty rooms automatically clean up ho jayein

title: Day 3 - Signaling Event Relay Implementation
body:
## 📌 Objective
Signaling ke core socket events (`offer`, `answer`, `ice-candidate`) ke listener aur relay events implement karein taaki targets socket connection par parameters sahi relay hon.

## ✅ Tasks
- [ ] `offer` socket event listener implement karein
- [ ] `answer` socket event listener implement karein
- [ ] `ice-candidate` socket event listener implement karein
- [ ] Events ko room ke dusre peer tak correctly relay karne ka logic likhein
- [ ] Sender ko khud apna event return na ho (`socket.to(roomId).emit()`)
- [ ] Room membership validate karein relay se pehle
- [ ] Invalid/malformed signaling payloads ke liye error handling add karein

## 🎯 Acceptance Criteria
- Peer A ka `offer` event Peer B tak relay ho
- Peer B ka `answer` event Peer A tak relay ho
- ICE candidates dono peers ke beech sahi relay hon
- Sender ko apna khud ka event wapas na mile
- Room se bahar ka koi user signaling events relay na kar sake

title: Day 4 - Frontend-Backend Integration
body:
## 📌 Objective
Frontend React app ko backend Socket se connect karein. Server and client connection logs verify karein, check karein ki room entry restrictions aur limits sahi chal rahi hain.

## ✅ Tasks
- [ ] `socket.io-client` React frontend mein install karein
- [ ] Socket connection hook banayein (`useSocket.js`)
- [ ] Frontend se backend Socket server se connection establish karein
- [ ] Room join flow UI se trigger karein aur socket event bhejein
- [ ] Server-side connection logs verify karein (terminal mein)
- [ ] Client-side connection confirmation log karein (browser console)
- [ ] Room full restriction test karein: teen window kholo, teesra block ho
- [ ] `REACT_APP_SERVER_URL` environment variable configure karein

## 🎯 Acceptance Criteria
- React app Socket server se successfully connect ho
- Browser console mein connection confirmation dikh rahi ho
- Server terminal mein client connection logs aa rahein hon
- Room join 2 users tak simit ho — teesra user block ho
- Room ID frontend se backend tak correctly pass ho

title: Day 5 - Logging & WebRTC Helper Configuration
body:
## 📌 Objective
Real-time debug logging set up karein. WebSocket packets ki structure integrity check karein aur external STUN configurations me design help karein.

## ✅ Tasks
- [ ] Structured logging library add karein (`morgan` ya `winston`)
- [ ] Socket event logging middleware implement karein (event name, room, socket ID)
- [ ] WebSocket packet structure validator likhein (payload fields check karein)
- [ ] STUN server configuration object define karein (Google/Cloudflare STUN)
- [ ] ICEServers config ko environment-aware banayein (dev vs prod STUN URLs)
- [ ] Log levels configure karein (debug, info, warn, error)
- [ ] Server startup logs meaningful banayein (port, env, socket status)

## 🎯 Acceptance Criteria
- Har socket event log ho (event type, roomId, socketId, timestamp)
- Malformed WebSocket packets caught aur logged hon
- STUN server config correctly defined aur accessible ho
- Log levels environment ke hisaab se switch karein
- Server startup clearly config details print kare

title: Day 6 - E2E WebRTC SDP Handshake Testing
body:
## 📌 Objective
Full WebRTC SDP Offer-Answer handshake test karein, remote streams trace karein aur local loopback connections test karein.

## ✅ Tasks
- [ ] Peer A se SDP Offer generate aur server ko bhejein
- [ ] Peer B tak Offer relay verify karein
- [ ] Peer B se SDP Answer generate aur relay karein
- [ ] Peer A tak Answer delivery verify karein
- [ ] ICE candidates exchange trace karein (dono peers ke liye)
- [ ] Local media stream (`getUserMedia`) capture aur display test karein
- [ ] Remote stream Peer B par display hona verify karein
- [ ] Local loopback connection test karein (ek tab mein dono peers simulate)
- [ ] `RTCPeerConnection` state transitions log karein (`new → connecting → connected`)

## 🎯 Acceptance Criteria
- SDP Offer-Answer cycle successfully complete ho
- ICE candidates dono peers ke beech exchange ho
- Local video stream browser mein dikh raha ho
- Remote video stream dusre peer par dikh raha ho
- `RTCPeerConnection` state `connected` tak pahunche
- Loopback test mein media successfully flow kare

title: Day 7 - Cross-Browser Testing & Phase 2 Preparation
body:
## 📌 Objective
Alag-alag browsers (Chrome vs Firefox/Safari) par check karein aur dynamic environment parameters check karke Phase 2 ke liye codebase ready karein.

## ✅ Tasks
- [ ] Chrome mein full handshake aur video call test karein
- [ ] Firefox mein full handshake aur video call test karein
- [ ] Safari mein basic compatibility check karein
- [ ] Chrome ↔ Firefox cross-browser call test karein
- [ ] Browser-specific WebRTC API differences document karein
- [ ] Dynamic environment variables verify karein (dev, staging, prod configs)
- [ ] Codebase refactor: unused code clean karein, TODOs resolve karein
- [ ] `README.md` update karein with Phase 1 status aur Phase 2 next steps
- [ ] Git repository clean karein aur Phase 1 tag lagayein (`v1.0-phase1`)

## 🎯 Acceptance Criteria
- Chrome mein video call successfully kaam kare
- Firefox mein video call successfully kaam kare
- Chrome ↔ Firefox cross-browser call kaam kare
- Safari mein koi critical blocker na ho
- Dynamic env config correctly load ho (dev vs prod)
- Codebase Phase 2 development ke liye ready ho
- README Phase 1 status clearly document kare
